name: NAM Framework 1961 (Non-Aligned Movement)
summary: The Non-Aligned Movement (NAM) is a global supranational meta-framework non-treaty informal-cooperation framework adopted by the 25 founding Global-South states in Belgrade — structurally distinctive globally as the only modern Global-South non-treaty supranational framework operating on formal non-aligned-Cold-War-era framework + largest Global-South supranational framework after UN framework (NAM First NAM Summit 1-6 September 1961 in Belgrade Yugoslavia by 25 founding states + NAM founding inspired by 1955 Bandung Conference + Tito + Nehru + Nasser + Nkrumah + Sukarno NAM founding leaders framework — formal foundational NAM framework establishing comprehensive NAM organisation framework operating non-treaty informal-cooperation framework, distinct from rest-of-modern-supranational-frameworks which operate as formal binding-treaty frameworks, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern Global-South non-treaty supranational framework + only modern supranational framework operating formal non-aligned-Cold-War-era framework + largest Global-South supranational framework after UN with 120 member states framework + ~55% global population framework + ~25% global GDP framework + only modern supranational framework operating formal Bandung-Conference 1955-precursor framework + only modern supranational framework operating formal Pancha Shila Five Principles of Peaceful Coexistence framework + only modern supranational framework operating formal post-Cold-War-continuation framework despite end of Cold War 1991), as the central jurisdiction of the formal NAM Bandung Conference 1955 Five Principles framework (Bandung Conference adopted 18-24 April 1955 in Bandung Indonesia by 29 Asian + African states adopting formal Pancha Shila Five Principles of Peaceful Coexistence + Bandung Declaration 10 Principles — formal foundational NAM-precursor framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern supranational framework operating formal Bandung-Conference 1955-precursor framework + formal Pancha Shila Five Principles of Peaceful Coexistence framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal NAM Belgrade Declaration 1961 framework (NAM Belgrade Declaration adopted 6 September 1961 at First NAM Summit Belgrade Yugoslavia providing formal NAM-non-aligned-foreign-policy framework — formal foundational NAM-non-aligned-foreign-policy framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern supranational framework operating formal NAM-non-aligned-foreign-policy framework), and as the central jurisdiction of the formal NAM Chairmanship + Summit Cycle framework (NAM Chairmanship rotates every 3-4 years + NAM Summit held every 3-4 years framework — formal foundational NAM Chairmanship + Summit framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern supranational framework operating formal triennial-quadrennial Chairmanship + Summit framework). The NAM Framework is operating upon 120 NAM member states + 17 NAM observer states + 10 NAM observer international organisations as of 2026 — comprehensive Global-South coverage framework. The Framework is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label, though formal NAM framework operates limited intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal Global-South cross-border-children's-rights framework not directly addressing family-law-issues framework. The NAM Summit + NAM Chairmanship + NAM Coordinating Bureau + NAM Ministerial Meeting are the formal NAM bodies; national-level apex courts operate as judicial bodies. The NAM Framework is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label.

statutory_framework.title: Bandung Conference
statutory_framework.relevance: Bandung Conference adopted 18-24 April 1955 in Bandung Indonesia by 29 Asian + African states — formal foundational NAM-precursor framework adopting Pancha Shila Five Principles of Peaceful Coexistence + Bandung Declaration 10 Principles.

statutory_framework.title: NAM Belgrade Declaration
statutory_framework.relevance: NAM Belgrade Declaration adopted 6 September 1961 at First NAM Summit Belgrade Yugoslavia — formal foundational NAM framework + NAM-non-aligned-foreign-policy framework.

statutory_framework.citation: Pancha Shila Five Principles of Peaceful Coexistence
statutory_framework.title: Pancha Shila
statutory_framework.relevance: Pancha Shila Five Principles of Peaceful Coexistence adopted 29 April 1954 in Sino-Indian Agreement + reaffirmed Bandung 1955 — formal foundational NAM-precursor framework.

statutory_framework.title: NAM Cartagena Document
statutory_framework.relevance: NAM Cartagena Document adopted 18-20 October 1995 at 11th NAM Summit Cartagena Colombia — formal NAM-post-Cold-War-continuation framework.

apex_courts.name: NAM Summit
apex_courts.role: Formal supreme decision-making body for NAM.

apex_courts.name: NAM Chairmanship
apex_courts.role: Formal NAM Chairmanship body rotating every 3-4 years.

apex_courts.name: NAM Coordinating Bureau
apex_courts.role: Formal NAM Coordinating Bureau body at UN.

apex_courts.name: NAM Ministerial Meeting
apex_courts.role: Formal foreign ministers body for NAM.

professional_regulators.name: NAM Chairmanship Secretariat
professional_regulators.role: NAM Framework implementation framework — rotates with NAM Chairmanship every 3-4 years.

anonymisation_convention: NAM-related communications operate via NAM Summit + NAM Chairmanship framework; individual communications not formally accepted under NAM framework.

key_developments.title: Pancha Shila Five Principles
key_developments.description: Pancha Shila Five Principles of Peaceful Coexistence adopted 29 April 1954 in Sino-Indian Agreement — predecessor framework to NAM.

key_developments.title: Bandung Conference
key_developments.description: Bandung Conference adopted 18-24 April 1955 in Bandung Indonesia by 29 Asian + African states — formal foundational NAM-precursor framework adopted.

key_developments.title: First NAM Summit Belgrade
key_developments.description: First NAM Summit held 1-6 September 1961 in Belgrade Yugoslavia by 25 founding states + NAM Belgrade Declaration adopted 6 September 1961 — formal foundational NAM framework operational.

key_developments.title: Egypt's expulsion + Israel-Egypt Peace Treaty
key_developments.description: Egypt's expulsion from NAM 1979 following Egypt-Israel Camp David Accords + Israel-Egypt Peace Treaty — formal NAM expulsion framework operational.

key_developments.title: Cold War ends + NAM relevance question framework
key_developments.description: Cold War ended 26 December 1991 with Soviet Union dissolution + NAM relevance question framework — formal NAM post-Cold-War-relevance question framework.

key_developments.title: NAM Cartagena Document
key_developments.description: NAM Cartagena Document adopted 18-20 October 1995 at 11th NAM Summit Cartagena Colombia — formal NAM-post-Cold-War-continuation framework adopted.

key_developments.title: Egypt rejoins NAM
key_developments.description: Egypt rejoined NAM 16 September 2006 at 14th NAM Summit Havana Cuba — formal NAM Egypt-rejoining framework.

key_developments.title: Venezuela hosts 17th NAM Summit
key_developments.description: 17th NAM Summit held 17-18 September 2016 in Margarita Island Venezuela — formal NAM Venezuela Chairmanship framework.

key_developments.title: Azerbaijan hosts 18th NAM Summit
key_developments.description: 18th NAM Summit held 25-26 October 2019 in Baku Azerbaijan + Azerbaijan Chairmanship 2019-2024 — formal NAM Azerbaijan Chairmanship framework.

key_developments.title: Uganda hosts 19th NAM Summit
key_developments.description: 19th NAM Summit held 15-20 January 2024 in Kampala Uganda + Uganda Chairmanship 2024-2027 — formal NAM Uganda Chairmanship framework.

structural_findings: NAM Framework operates a supranational non-treaty informal-cooperation framework — places NAM Framework in the global supranational-meta-framework cluster.

Only modern Global-South non-treaty supranational framework operating on formal non-aligned-Cold-War-era framework + largest Global-South supranational framework after UN framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern Global-South non-treaty supranational framework + only modern supranational framework operating formal non-aligned-Cold-War-era framework + largest Global-South supranational framework after UN with 120 member states + ~55% global population + ~25% global GDP + only modern supranational framework operating formal Bandung-Conference 1955-precursor framework + only modern supranational framework operating formal Pancha Shila Five Principles of Peaceful Coexistence framework + only modern supranational framework operating formal post-Cold-War-continuation framework despite end of Cold War 1991.

Central jurisdiction of formal NAM Bandung Conference 1955 Five Principles framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern supranational framework operating formal Bandung-Conference 1955-precursor framework + formal Pancha Shila Five Principles of Peaceful Coexistence framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal NAM Belgrade Declaration 1961 framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern supranational framework operating formal NAM-non-aligned-foreign-policy framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal NAM Chairmanship + Summit Cycle framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern supranational framework operating formal triennial-quadrennial Chairmanship + Summit framework.
120 NAM member state + 17 NAM observer state + 10 NAM observer international organisation ratification framework is structurally distinctive globally — comprehensive Global-South coverage framework.

1954 Pancha Shila + 1955 Bandung Conference + 1961 First NAM Summit + 1979 Egypt expulsion + 1991 Cold War ends + 1995 Cartagena Document + 2006 Egypt rejoins + 2016 Venezuela + 2019 Azerbaijan + 2024 Uganda NAM-framework-development sequence is structurally distinctive globally.

NAM Framework + Bandung Conference + Pancha Shila + NAM Belgrade Declaration + Cartagena Document formal quintet framework with limited PA-intersection framework via formal Global-South cross-border-children's-rights framework not directly addressing family-law-issues framework — distinctive in operating non-treaty informal-cooperation framework relative to other regional + global frameworks.
editorial_notes: NAM Framework jurisdiction sidecar — supranational global Global-South non-treaty informal-cooperation meta-framework (NAM Framework 1961 Belgrade + Bandung Conference 1955 + Pancha Shila 1954 + NAM Belgrade Declaration 1961 + Cartagena Document 1995 + 1979 Egypt expulsion + 2006 Egypt rejoins + 1991 Cold War ends + 2019 Azerbaijan Chairmanship + 2024 Uganda Chairmanship). Only modern Global-South non-treaty supranational framework globally + largest Global-South supranational framework after UN with 120 member states + ~55% global population + ~25% global GDP + only modern supranational framework operating formal non-aligned-Cold-War-era framework + only modern supranational framework operating formal Bandung-Conference 1955-precursor framework + only modern supranational framework operating formal Pancha Shila Five Principles of Peaceful Coexistence framework + only modern supranational framework operating formal post-Cold-War-continuation framework + central jurisdiction of formal Bandung Conference 1955 Five Principles + central jurisdiction of formal Belgrade Declaration 1961 NAM-non-aligned-foreign-policy + central jurisdiction of formal triennial-quadrennial Chairmanship + Summit + 120 NAM member states + 17 observer states + 10 observer international organisations + 1954 Pancha Shila + 1955 Bandung Conference + 1961 First NAM Summit + 1979 Egypt expulsion + 1991 Cold War ends + 1995 Cartagena Document + 2006 Egypt rejoins + 2016 Venezuela + 2019 Azerbaijan + 2024 Uganda NAM-framework-development sequence.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator. NOTE: NAM framework operates limited intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal Global-South cross-border-children's-rights framework not directly addressing family-law-issues framework — distinctive in operating non-treaty informal-cooperation framework relative to other regional + global frameworks.

Joins global + supranational + Global-South-non-treaty-informal-cooperation cluster + NAM-Framework-only-Global-South-non-treaty-supranational-framework-globally-distinctive + largest-Global-South-supranational-after-UN-120-members + 55-percent-global-population + 25-percent-global-GDP + Bandung-Conference-1955-precursor + Pancha-Shila-Five-Principles-of-Peaceful-Coexistence-1954 + Belgrade-Declaration-1961 + Tito-Nehru-Nasser-Nkrumah-Sukarno-founding-leaders + Cartagena-Document-1995-post-Cold-War-continuation + 1979-Egypt-expulsion-Camp-David + 2006-Egypt-rejoins-Havana + 1991-Cold-War-ends + NAM-Coordinating-Bureau-New-York + 19-NAM-Summits-1961-2024 + 2019-Azerbaijan-Baku-Chairmanship + 2024-Uganda-Kampala-Chairmanship + 17-NAM-observer-states + 10-NAM-observer-international-organisations + limited-PA-intersection-via-Global-South-non-treaty-framework clusters within the corpus.
